content: Just as the human fan effect predicts slower associative recognition when a concept is linked to more memory traces, the working memory capacity limit of AI agents can be formalized as a system-level fan effect, where adding more associative connections to active context creates predictable non-linear slowdowns in reasoning speed and accuracy. This framing explains why even large-context AI agents suffer unexpected reasoning failures when asked to draw inferences across many related facts stored in active working memory.
source_topics: agent working memory capacity limits reasoning
